mnemonics: DRAFT For Better Optimization: Define variables, Represent constraints, Arrange in standard form, Formulate the objective, Test with Simplex.
keyPoints: The Simplex Method is an algorithm used to solve linear programming problems by moving from one vertex of the feasible region to an adjacent one with an improved objective value.
Duality theory states that every linear programming problem (the primal) has an associated dual problem, and the optimal values of both are equal under certain conditions.
Sensitivity Analysis (or post-optimality analysis) examines how the optimal solution changes with variations in the coefficients of the objective function or the constraints.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to convert inequality constraints to equalities by adding slack, surplus, or artificial variables when setting up the Simplex tableau.
Misinterpreting the signs of the dual variables or incorrectly formulating the dual problem from its primal counterpart.
Assuming the optimal solution is infinitely stable and neglecting to perform sensitivity analysis on the initial model's coefficients.
